chapter_title.ar: باب مَنْ سَهَا فِي الْأَرْبَعِ وَ الْخَمْسِ وَ لَمْ يَدْرِ زَادَ أَوْ نَقَصَ أَوِ اسْتَيْقَنَ أَنَّهُ زَادَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 41 – The one who omits in the fourth and the fifth and does not know he has exceeded or is deficient, or he is convinced that he has exceeded

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/201)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/201)
text: قَالَ مَنْ حَفِظَ سَهْوَهُ وَ أَتَمَّهُ فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ سَجْدَتَا السَّهْوِ إِنَّمَا السَّهْوُ عَلَى مَنْ لَمْ يَدْرِ زَادَ أَمْ نَقَصَ مِنْهَا .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from Usman Bin Isa, from Sama’at who said,

‘The one who safeguards his omission and completes it, so there are no two Sujūd of the omission (Sajd Al-Sah’w) upon him. But rather, the omission is upon the one who does not know whether he increased or reduced from it’.
en.sarwar: 4. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from ‘Uthman ibn ‘Isa from Sama‘ah who has said the following: “He (the Imam), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If one knows the kind of mistake he has made and completes it then Sajdah (prostration) for mistake is not necessary for him. Sajdah (prostration) for mistake is upon one who does not know if he has increased or decreased something in Salah (prayer).”’
